## Executive Summary
The retrieved documents provide some information about the associations between David Ferrie, Clay Shaw, and Guy Banister in New Orleans, but the details are limited and scattered across multiple sources. The records mention Clay Shaw's connections to the CIA and his involvement in the World Trade Center, as well as David Ferrie's association with the New Orleans office of the DCS, but they do not directly describe the relationships between these individuals [1] [2]. The documents also mention Guy Banister's involvement in anti-Castro activities, but his connections to Ferrie and Shaw are not explicitly stated.

## Detailed Findings
### Clay Shaw and the CIA
The documents reveal that Clay Shaw had a relationship with the CIA, although the nature of this relationship is not fully explained [1]. A memorandum from C/EUR/I to C/CI/R & A, dated 8 March 1967, discusses the Italian aspects of the Clay Shaw affair, including his involvement with the World Trade Center [1]. Another document mentions that Shaw was granted a covert security approval in 1962, but the purpose of this approval is not specified [2].

### David Ferrie and the DCS
David Ferrie is mentioned in connection with the New Orleans office of the DCS, where he was associated with Jack S. Martin, who was working on an undercover deal for Garrison [2]. However, the documents do not provide further details about Ferrie's activities or his relationships with Shaw or Banister.

### Guy Banister and Anti-Castro Activities
Guy Banister is mentioned in the context of anti-Castro activities, but his connections to Ferrie and Shaw are not explicitly stated [3]. The documents do mention that Banister was involved in the investigation of the Kennedy assassination, but the details of his involvement are not provided.

### New Orleans Associations
The records do not directly describe the associations between Ferrie, Shaw, and Banister in New Orleans. However, they do mention that Shaw was the managing director of the International Trade Mart in New Orleans, and that Ferrie was associated with the New Orleans office of the DCS [1] [2]. The documents also mention that Banister was involved in anti-Castro activities in New Orleans, but the connections to Ferrie and Shaw are not specified [3].
